description: The gift: what changes hands with nothing owed back. The verb 'loa' (give) opens the word, so the noun carries its verb visibly. Distinct from a trade; the gift's whole meaning is that the ledger stays closed.
sound_symbolism: The complete verb 'loa' (give) begins the word, already enacting the open-handed transfer. Closing 'mi' makes the thing intimate and held, a gift received into someone's keeping without opening a ledger.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'mia loami loa.' (I give a gift.) 'lopia loami howela.' (The child receives a gift.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Generosity is the first perfection in the old lists, and the gift is its object: giving practiced until the hand opens before the mind counts.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Gift economies are older than markets. Whole societies ran on what this word names: obligation converted into relationship.
